Atomic Skills product docs
Home Skills Project Modules Hosts

← Home

Project skill domain dataset

Project skill — mental model

Plan ⊃ Phase ⊃ Task tracking for multi-day work, reloaded every session.

NO IMPLEMENTATION WITHOUT AN ANCHORED INITIATIVE.

The project skill keeps Plan / Initiative / Task state in .atomic-skills/ so every session reloads the exact frame — stack frames for side work, an emergence ladder for new work, exit gates before advancing, and one git-style command surface for the whole lifecycle.

Canonical state lives in .atomic-skills/ (git-tracked; never hand-edited). Command prefix: /atomic-skills:project. This page is generated from meta/product/project-guide.yaml — not from catalog core.project bloat.

Entities

Three-level hierarchy plus inbox and side frames.

Entity What it is Nests
Plan Multi-phase delivery with one objective and its own branch (plan/<slug>). Phases (initiatives)
Phase / Initiative A plan stage (F0, F1, …) with an exit-gate. A standalone initiative is a one-phase plan. Tasks
Task Implementable unit (T-001, …) with acceptance criteria and a verifier. Leaf of the tree
Ideas Raw inbox in ideas.md before work is structured. Outside the tree; enters via idea promote
Stack frames Side investigations stacked on current work (push / pop). Temporary stack
Backlog Future work in parked[] / emerged[] classified by the emergence ladder. Becomes a Task via promote

Key concepts

Lazy materialization

new plan creates plan.md and materializes only F0. F1..N stay descriptor-only until materialize <phase>. descriptor-only = line in the plan, no initiative file yet. materializada = full initiative file with tasks, ready for implement.

businessIntent

HARD gate at materialize. Captures value / workflow / rules / outOfScope / doneWhen. A phase does not materialize without this spine.

Exit-gate

Each phase has verifiable exit criteria. You do not advance while a criterion is pending. Criteria close via verifier (shell / test / query), not guesswork.

Lifecycle spine

Work follows one gated spine from idea to archive. You do not advance a stage with an open question.

  1. IDEA Capture in ideas.md or park/emerge →
  2. DESIGN brainstorm → approved design.md →
  3. PLAN Source plan markdown + no-placeholder lint →
  4. DECOMPOSE Phases + tasks; SPEC gate (4 HOW fields) →
  5. MATERIALIZE businessIntent HARD gate →
  6. IMPLEMENT Task-by-task via atomic-skills:implement →
  7. VERIFY done runs verifier; evidence required →
  8. REVIEW phase-done requires code review →
  9. DONE Phase or plan complete →
  10. ARCHIVE finalize → PR; then archive

Execution loop (per phase)

  • materialize <phase>
    businessIntent
  • implement (task by task)
    SPEC admission (Files, scopeBoundary, acceptance, deterministic verifier)
  • done <task-id>
    Verifier must pass; rewrites nextAction
  • phase-done
    Exit-gate verifiers + mandatory code review → advances currentPhase

Can / cannot

What the skill does

  • Reload the exact multi-day frame each session (phase, parked work, why a task exists).
  • Anchor every code-changing session to an active initiative (or explicit ad-hoc).
  • Classify new work on the emergence ladder before it lands (park / emerge / promote).
  • Stack side investigations without losing the main thread (push / pop).
  • Block phase advance until exit-gate criteria pass.
  • Detect scope-creep and state-vs-code drift.
  • One git-style command for view, create, mutate, discover, migrate, verify.

What it does not allow

  • Iron Law — no implementation without an anchored initiative (or explicit ad-hoc).
  • Never hand-edit .atomic-skills/; subcommands set provenance and validate.
  • Do not decompose a plan whose design.md fails lint.
  • No task admitted to implement without the four HOW fields and a deterministic verifier (manual does not count).
  • No placeholders (REPLACE_* / TODO / TBD / FIXME / <path>) past the HARD gate — nothing is written until clean.
  • reconcile is the only completion-mutation path for “looks done” tasks/gates.
  • review never closes a task or advances a phase — report only.
  • Do not advance a phase with a pending gate criterion (unless deferred with an explicit reason).
  • Do not advance a plan blocked by dependsOnPlans[].
  • Legacy files without schemaVersion must migrate before any mutation.
  • Not for one-shot questions or work that fits the current session alone.

Command reference

All commands share the prefix /atomic-skills:project. Bare /atomic-skills:project prints a compact 5-line summary and stops (no browser).

View

  • /atomic-skills:project status [--browser|--terminal|--list|--plan|--phase|--stack|--archived|--report]
    Compact summary, aiDeck dashboard, full terminal view, or filtered tables.
  • /atomic-skills:project verify [--fix] [--slug <slug>]
    Reconcile .atomic-skills/ vs repo (schema, branch, scope, orphans). Read-only unless --fix.

Create

  • /atomic-skills:project new [plan|initiative] <slug>
    Bootstrap a multi-phase plan (gated flow) or a standalone/anchored initiative.
  • /atomic-skills:project discover [--dry-run|--commit] [--scope=<list>] [--scan=<path>]
    Scan the repo and propose Plans + Initiatives for approve/reject.
  • /atomic-skills:project adopt <file.md>
    Capture freeform markdown into structured Plan + Initiatives + Tasks.

Stack frames

  • /atomic-skills:project push <description>
    Open a side frame on current work (type inferred from the verb).
  • /atomic-skills:project pop [--resolve|--park|--emerge]
    Close the top frame — discard, note, or follow-up.

Backlog (emergence ladder)

  • /atomic-skills:project park <description>
    Low-commitment note in parked[] (ratification gate for solves/trigger).
  • /atomic-skills:project emerge <description>
    Real follow-up in emerged[]; optional --target <phaseId>.
  • /atomic-skills:project promote <title-or-idx>
    Turn a parked item into a real task (next T-NNN).
  • /atomic-skills:project idea [list | promote <n>]
    Raw ideas.md inbox; promote routes through the emergence ladder.

Tasks & phases

  • /atomic-skills:project done <task-id>
    Close a task with closedAt; may offer phase-done or archive if last open.
  • /atomic-skills:project reconcile
    Close tasks/gates that look done in the repo — only completion-mutation path.
  • /atomic-skills:project materialize <phase-id>
    descriptor-only → full initiative; captures businessIntent HARD gate.
  • /atomic-skills:project unblock <task-id>
    Return a blocked task to workable state (does not close it).
  • /atomic-skills:project phase-done
    Verify exit-gate criteria, mandatory code review, advance currentPhase.
  • /atomic-skills:project phase-reopen [<phase-id>]
    Revert phase-done; initiative active again; criteria/tasks reset.
  • /atomic-skills:project split-phase <id>
    Split an oversized phase; original archived (not done) with provenance.

Lifecycle

  • /atomic-skills:project finalize
    Publish plan branch as PR (push plan/<slug> + gh pr create).
  • /atomic-skills:project consolidate
    1:N merge-train of ready worktrees into an integration branch + PR.
  • /atomic-skills:project archive [<slug>]
    Move finished plan/initiative to archive/ (plan cascades to phases).
  • /atomic-skills:project switch <slug>
    Pause current plan/initiative and activate the target.
  • /atomic-skills:project migrate [<slug>]
    Legacy schema conversion or flat→projects/<id>/<slug>/ layout cut-over.
  • /atomic-skills:project re-bootstrap <slug>
    After migrate, re-articulate parked/emerged items still holding placeholders.

Context & drift

  • /atomic-skills:project why <id>
    Deep read-only view — status, solves/trigger/assumptions, provenance, staleness.
  • /atomic-skills:project re-ratify <id>
    Refresh a stale item — reconfirm premises or rewrite solves/trigger/assumptions.
  • /atomic-skills:project scope-creep
    Read-only drift report (phase growth, scope expansion, parked zombies, stale context).
  • /atomic-skills:project detect-scope
    Suggest scope.paths from recent git activity as an accept checklist.

Review

  • /atomic-skills:project review [<slug>] [--with-code] [--mode=local|both]
    Mutation-gated adversarial audit; reports findings only — never closes work.
  • /atomic-skills:project review-due
    Cross-model review of diff since last review; records the result.

Dependencies

  • /atomic-skills:project depend list | add <dependent> <prerequisite> | remove … | resolve … --archived
    Manage dependsOnPlans[] (dashboard lanes Liberado / Em andamento / Bloqueado / Concluido).

Sister skills

Project is a thin orchestrator — it delegates parts of the cycle.

Moment Skill Role
DESIGN (new plan stage 2) atomic-skills:brainstorm Critic-approved design.md before decompose
Adversarial divergence atomic-skills:debate Independent design voices
Post-materialize execution atomic-skills:implement Single-threaded driver of admitted tasks to DONE
Task / gate close atomic-skills:verify-claim Fresh-run evidence for done/pass/fixed claims
review / phase-done atomic-skills:review-plan + review-code Adversarial plan and code audit
Bug mid-flight atomic-skills:fix Root-cause + TDD fix
Live project state aiDeck (status --browser) Dashboard graphs, exit-gates, live SSE

Lost?

Terminal GPS
/atomic-skills:project help

Where you are and what next, from live .atomic-skills/ state

Live dashboard
/atomic-skills:project status --browser

Living state — not this static guide

Bare status
/atomic-skills:project

Compact 5-line summary; does not open a browser

Generated from meta/catalog.yaml, src/config.js, and optional meta/product/*.yaml datasets. Product HTML is a build view only — not the SSOT.
